column1: # Echo Speaker — staging rows for merge

column3: description

column1: # === L1 Core fills (2) — mobility + passive gaps ===

column3: Slip sideways through the Veil. Move a short distance as if the terrain between living and dead were the same map; ignore intervening obstacles this step.
The iron-dead never stop whispering to you. Passive: at the start of each encounter, gain one grief stack; enemies that enter stealth near you are heard anyway.
The dead taught you their syntax. Your next RESONANCE or VEIL check this turn can be made in either stat; use whichever is higher.
You have learned to carry more of the afterlife at once. Your grief-stack ceiling is raised, and when you would gain a stack you instead gain two.

column1: # === L3 Sutensai Aligned (4) — void-channel, erasure, empty-vowel speech ===

column3: Speak a sound that is not a sound. One enemy's next action is partially unwritten; they lose either their movement or their attack this turn, their choice.
You take the target's name from the air for a heartbeat. Until end of their next turn, no ally of the target may buff or heal them — they do not exist to their own side.
Channel a voice that has no speaker. Make a VEIL attack that ignores armor but cannot score a critical; the target forgets what hit them and cannot identify your position on their next turn.
Ask the void a question and accept its reply. Discard one card; name any enemy in the encounter — you learn one true fact the GM was hiding about them (affiliation, weakness, or hidden card).

column1: # === L3 Deep Listener (4) — iron-afterlife translation, dead's counsel, memory of wounds ===

column3: Press your ear to the battlefield; the dead tell you what's coming. Peek at the top two cards of one enemy's action queue and may force them to discard one.
Every injury is a sentence the dead can read. Touch a wounded ally; you learn who dealt the wound and your next attack against that enemy strikes with advantage.
Speak a dead warrior's last words into an ally's hand. Choose one ally; they may immediately play one of their discarded cards as if freshly drawn, at 1 AP less (min 1).

Open your chest to every voice in the Iron Afterlife at once. VEIL-weighted area effect; all enemies in earshot are deafened and made legible — your allies' attacks against them this round gain advantage.

column1: # === L3 Herald (4) — mouthpiece of power, declarative speech, speaks things into being ===

column3: State an injury aloud and the world agrees. Point at an enemy and name a damage type; they take minor damage of that type immediately, and their resistance to it is ignored for one turn.
Pronounce the stop. One enemy is rooted by your word until the start of your next turn, or until an ally strikes them — whichever comes first.
Call an ally by the name they will earn. That ally's next card this round is played at 1 less AP (min 1) and cannot be reacted to.
Announce the outcome before it happens. Choose one enemy at full health or nearly so; they become Called. While Called, the first critical dealt to them this encounter is automatically maximized.

column1: # === L5 Power (2 more; class already has Veil Apotheosis) ===

column2: The Word That Was a Blade
column3: Speak a single syllable the iron-dead have been saving for you. VEIL+RESONANCE strike against one enemy; if it drops them, every grief stack you hold transfers as damage to the nearest remaining enemy.
You become a channel instead of a speaker. Until the start of your next turn, every ally within earshot may spend your grief stacks instead of their own AP to play one card each.
Unspeak the moment. The last enemy action resolved this round is retroactively undone — damage un-dealt, movement reversed, card returned to hand. The void takes the memory of it instead.
Open a void where your voice was. For two rounds, enemies who target you must first pass a RESONANCE check or lose the target entirely — they forget who they were aiming at.

Every iron-dead warrior within reach speaks at once through you. Summon a choir of spectral advisors; until end of encounter, you and allies may treat enemy actions as if you had seen them one card in advance.

You listen past the battle to the pact that holds the Iron Afterlife open. Name any enemy in the encounter; you learn their true purpose here, and one bond they cannot break without breaking themselves — exploiting it this turn is devastating.

Declare a condition of the battlefield and make it so. Name one of: an ally is at full health, a wall stands here, this enemy has no weapon. The statement is true for one round; the world corrects afterward.

Pronounce an ally the hero of this encounter. For the rest of the fight, their critical range is widened and every enemy that strikes them first tests against your VEIL — failure means their blow simply does not land.
